name: Grilled Pork Tacos & Papaya Salsa
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------PAPAYA SALSA-----------------------
1. Peel, seed, and cut papaya into half-inch cubes.
2. Chop red chili.
3. Chop red onion.
4. Chop red bell pepper.
5. Chop fresh mint leaves.
6. Squeeze lime juice.
-----------------------PORK MIXTURE-----------------------
1. Cut pork (center loin) into 1/4-inch strips.
2. Chop fresh papaya.
3. Chop fresh pineapple.
4. Prepare 10 flour tortillas (6-7 inches in diameter).
5. Shred Monterey Jack cheese (6 oz).
6. Melt margarine or butter.
Cook the pork in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until no longer pink. Drain and stir in papaya and pineapple. Heat until hot.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Place about 1/4 cup of the pork mixture onto half of each tortilla. Top with about 2 tablespoons of cheese. Fold tortillas over the filling.
Arrange five filled tortillas in an ungreased jelly roll pan (15.5x10.5 inches). Brush with melted margarine.
Bake uncovered for about 10 minutes or until light golden brown. Repeat with remaining tacos.
Serve with papaya salsa. Makes 5 servings.
Salsa: Mix all ingredients. Cover and refrigerate for about 30 minutes or until chilled. Makes 3 cups salsa.
